title: Analyzing Swiatek's Coaching Split After Miami Open Setback
summary: Iga Swiatek, a six-time major tennis champion, has ended her coaching partnership with Wim Fissette following an early exit from the Miami Open in 2026. Fissette joined her team in October 2024 and contributed to significant successes, including her first Wimbledon title in July 2025 and additional WTA Tour wins in Seoul and Cincinnati. However, the 2026 season has been challenging for Swiatek, as she failed to reach beyond the quarter-finals in all four tournaments played, culminating in a second-round loss to compatriot Magda Linette at Miami. This marked her first opening-match defeat in 74 WTA Tour events. Swiatek expressed disappointment and responsibility on Instagram, stating she learned valuable lessons but decided to pursue a different path after months of collaboration. The article highlights how Fissette helped boost her inner belief through technical improvements like footwork and serve, yet consistency across tournaments remained elusive. Swiatek, described as an introvert, performs optimally with clarity and confidence, relying on baseline strategies to dominate opponents.

the ability to perform well regularly without big changes
changes to skills like footwork or serve to play better
playing from the back of the court to control points
a person who is quiet and may prefer being alone

background_read: Iga Swiatek is a Polish tennis star ranked world number three, known for winning six Grand Slam titles. Coaching in tennis involves strategic guidance and mental support, with changes often occurring after performance slumps. The WTA Tour includes global tournaments like the Miami Open, where players earn points and prizes. Athletes' personalities, such as being introverted, can affect their performance and coaching dynamics.
Article_Structure: This article analyzes Iga Swiatek's split from coach Wim Fissette after her Miami Open loss. Main Points: The partnership brought initial success with Wimbledon and other titles, but recent poor performance led to the decision. Purpose: To report on the coaching change and explore reasons behind it, including Swiatek's struggles in 2026. Evidence Evaluation: Uses specific tournament results, Swiatek's Instagram statement, and historical context of 74 tournaments without early loss, providing credible but limited insider perspectives. Author Credibility: Likely a sports journalist from BBC Tennis, offering factual reporting without deep analysis. Methodology: Relies on public statements, tournament data, and descriptive observations to construct the narrative.

perspectives.description: Focuses on how Swiatek's on-court results and consistency issues influenced the coaching decision.
